stimulus.content: In 1991, the Soviet Union broke apart into 15 separate countries, including Russia, Ukraine, and Kazakhstan. Before 1991, all of these areas were part of one large country. This shows that political borders can change over time.

stem: What does the breakup of the Soviet Union show about political borders?

choices.text: Country borders can change due to political events
Country borders are permanent and never change
Borders only change when natural disasters strike
Borders are always decided by geographic features

answer_explanation: The Soviet Union splitting into 15 countries in 1991 is a major example of how political borders can change due to political events, wars, or independence movements.
